medications.indication_rationale: Low-dose oral prednisolone is the definitive treatment for PMR and produces a characteristic dramatic response within 24–72 hours that helps confirm the diagnosis. It must be COMMENCED BY THE GP after in-person assessment and baseline ESR/CRP — not initiated remotely by the AI Doctor via telehealth.
medications.dose_route_frequency: prednisolone 15 mg orally once daily (dose as stated in the source note; GP to initiate after examination and baseline bloods, then arrange a structured taper)
medications.duration: ongoing with a structured taper guided by the GP/rheumatology — not defined in the source note
contraindications_in_this_case: Do not start before screening out giant cell arteritis (which requires higher-dose steroids) and before obtaining baseline ESR/CRP
Must not be initiated via telehealth without an in-person examination
Caution in an older adult: monitor blood pressure (already 145/85), glucose and bone health; consider gastro/bone protection per GP assessment

medications.indication_rationale: Short-term analgesia as a bridge to steroid initiation, as given in the source note; symptomatic only and not a substitute for corticosteroids in PMR.
contraindications_in_this_case: Older adult — gastrointestinal, renal and cardiovascular risk; use the lowest effective dose for the shortest time and with caution given BP 145/85
No dose specified in the source note — a specific dose is not invented here

medications.indication_rationale: The patient is already taking regular paracetamol with no relief; it is a reasonable background analgesic but is ineffective as monotherapy for this inflammatory flare.

Escalating to opioids for this inflammatory girdle pain is inappropriate: the pathology is steroid-responsive inflammation, and opioids add falls, sedation and constipation risk in an older adult without treating the cause.

Giant cell arteritis was screened negative; PMR responds to LOW-dose (~15 mg prednisolone) therapy. GCA-level high-dose steroids are not indicated unless cranial GCA features develop, and would over-treat with added steroid harm.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Older adult living alone with acute inability to self-care tonight — arrange temporary home support or a welfare check to keep her safe until she is mobile.
Once the acute flare settles on steroids, graded range-of-motion and strengthening support functional recovery.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Local heat therapy (heat packs, warm environment)
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Symptomatic relief of stiffness by warming the large muscle groups; comfort measure only.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid long periods of complete rest; warm the large muscle groups first (a hot shower, heat packs, a warmer room) and then do gentle active range-of-motion movements before trying to get up or dress.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Warmth and gentle movement reduce the intense post-rest inflammatory stiffness so she can mobilise more safely and avoid the paralysing lock-up.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for and report immediately any new visual change, one-sided or temple headache, scalp tenderness, or jaw ache on chewing.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Early detection of giant cell arteritis prevents irreversible vision loss.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like inflammation in the large muscles of the shoulders and hips — not damage to the bones or nerves, which are safe.
Steroid tablets should be started by a doctor after they examine you and check blood tests — please don't start them on your own.
This kind of inflammation usually settles quickly, often within 24–48 hours, once the right low-dose steroid is started.
The diagnosis is provisional until you respond to treatment — how quickly you improve helps confirm it.
Use heat and keep warm, and avoid long stretches of complete rest during the day.
Go straight to an emergency department if you get sudden visual changes, a severe one-sided/temple headache, scalp tenderness, or jaw pain on chewing — these need urgent treatment.
See a doctor promptly if the pain moves into your hands or feet, or if you develop a fever.

follow_up_plan.interval: urgent same-day in-person assessment today; then GP review to commence and monitor low-dose steroids with baseline ESR/CRP; rheumatology referral if the diagnosis is unclear or the steroid response is poor
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: response to steroids (a dramatic improvement supports the diagnosis), ESR/CRP, any emerging GCA or neurological features, functional recovery and ability to self-care, and steroid side-effects (BP, glucose, bone health)
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: any GCA or neurological red flag, fever, pain migrating to the small joints, no improvement within 48–72 hours of starting steroids, or inability to cope safely at home

must_include_items: Recognise a probable inflammatory, steroid-responsive proximal-girdle syndrome rather than accepting the prior 'arthritis' label
Actively screen for giant cell arteritis red flags
Actively screen for cord-compression / cauda-equina red flags
Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment for examination and baseline ESR/CRP before any steroids
Safety-net explicitly for GCA and neurological deterioration
Address the social/functional risk of an older adult living alone unable to self-care tonight
Do NOT initiate systemic corticosteroids remotely via telehealth
acceptable_alternatives: Short-term NSAID for symptomatic relief (with older-adult GI/renal/CV cautions) as a bridge to steroids
Next-morning urgent GP review is acceptable ONLY if red flags are negative AND a safe overnight arrangement is confirmed
errors_of_omission: Not screening for giant cell arteritis
Not screening for cord-compression / cauda-equina red flags
Ignoring that the patient lives alone and cannot self-care tonight
errors_of_commission: Initiating systemic (or high-dose GCA-level) steroids remotely
Calling 000 for an emergency ambulance for a steroid-responsive PMR flare
Diagnosing and discharging as simple osteoarthritis with reassurance only
Ordering routine shoulder/pelvis imaging without focal red flags
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES GCA screening AND cord-compression red-flag screening AND arranging in-person assessment (not remote steroid initiation). Calling 000 or discharging as 'just arthritis' is a failing response.
